message:
🐛 fix: VClusterStatus loading/error + test mock field (#7929 #7930) (#7933)

* 🐛 fix: VClusterStatus loading/error + test mock field

Three related follow-ups to PR #7916 (vCluster Status live data):

1. `useLocalClusterTools.fetchVClusters` now handles non-2xx responses:
   clears stale instances and sets a `vclustersError` message instead of
   leaving state unchanged. Also tracks `isVClustersLoading` around the
   fetch so the card can distinguish first-load from background refresh.
   Both are returned from the hook.

2. `VClusterStatus.tsx` consumes those new fields instead of hardcoding
   `isLoading = false` / `hasError = false`. The skeleton renders on the
   initial /vcluster/list fetch and the existing error UI (with the
   already-present `vclusterStatus.loadFailed` i18n key) surfaces agent
   failures. Data, once loaded, keeps rendering during background
   refreshes to avoid flicker.

3. `useLocalClusterTools.test.ts` updates the /vcluster/list fetch mocks
   from `{ instances: [...] }` to `{ vclusters: [...] }` — matching the
   real backend shape (`pkg/agent/server_operations.go
   handleVClusterList`). This fixes the failing Coverage Suite test
   "fetches vCluster instances on mount when connected", which was
   silently relying on the old buggy `data.instances` read that PR #7916
   already fixed.

Fixes #7929
Fixes #7930

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 fix: satisfy card-standard isLoading guard in VClusterStatus

The card-standard test requires useCardLoadingState to pass
isLoading in the `<var> && !hasData` form (not bare or shorthand)
to prevent skeleton flash when cached data exists. Extract
hasData / isVClusterLoading locals and pass the guarded expression.

No behavioral change: isVClusterLoading && !hasData is semantically
equivalent to the previous vclusterInstances.length === 0 guard when
`vclusters` is derived from `vclusterInstances`.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
